description: Romulus is the legendary founder linked to Rome's foundation myth.
The foundation myth introduces the traditional monarchic phase of Rome.
The Roman Kingdom precedes the traditional birth of the Republic.
The expulsion of the kings is the traditional origin of the Roman Republic.
The Senate was a central institution of Republican politics.
Hannibal was the central Carthaginian commander of the Second Punic War.
Cannae was one of the major battles of the Second Punic War.
Hannibal commanded the Carthaginian army at Cannae.
Scipio commanded the Roman forces at Zama.
Zama decided the war in Rome's favor.
Caesar's political conflict led to his crossing of the Rubicon.
The Rubicon crisis accelerated the collapse of the Republic.
Caesar's career and death opened the political path for Augustus.
Augustus kept Republican forms while building imperial rule.
